zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Braose於1224年出生在威爾士時，她的父親William de Braose28歲，母親Lady Eve Abergavenny21歲。她於大約1247年與Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1301年3月23日在英國賀福爾維格莫享年77歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾維格莫 崔德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Braose于1224年出生在威尔士时，她的父亲William de Braose28岁，母亲Lady Eve Abergavenny21岁。她于大约1247年与Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1301年3月23日在英国賀福爾維格莫享年77岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀福爾維格莫 崔德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Braose was born in 1224, in Wales, her father, William de Braose, was 28 and her mother, Lady Eve Abergavenny, was 21. She married Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore about 1247. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 23 March 1301, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Wigmore Hundred, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Braose est née en 1224, à Galles, son père, William de Braose, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Lady Eve Abergavenny, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore environ 1247. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 mars 1301, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Wigmore Hundred, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Braose 1224 in Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Braose 28 und ihre Mutter Lady Eve Abergavenny 21. Sie hat um 1247 Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 23. März 1301 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England und wurde in Wigmore Hundred, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Braoseが1224年にウェールズで生まれた時，父William de Braoseは28歳，母Lady Eve Abergavennyは21歳であった。約1247年にSir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmoreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1301年3月23日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで77歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモー ハンドレドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Braose(이)가 1224년 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Braose(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Eve Abergavenny(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1247년 Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1301년 3월 23일 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 위그모어 헌드레드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Braose nasceu em 1224, em Gales, seu pai, William de Braose, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Lady Eve Abergavenny, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore aproximadamente 1247. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de março de 1301, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Wigmore Hundred, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Braose родилась в 1224 г. в местности Уэльс, её отцу по имени William de Braose было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Eve Abergavenny, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore около 1247 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 23 марта 1301 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности вигмор хундред, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Braose nació en 1224, en Gales, su papá, William de Braose, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Lady Eve Abergavenny, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir Roger de Mortimer II, First Baron of Wigmore aproximadamente 1247. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de marzo de 1301, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Wigmore Hundred, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
